comment.body: I get that the negative vanna compresses downside and allows for upside volatility (can see this in the skews) so i understand how we go up. What i cant understand is why volatility hasn't been more sensitive given we're post June OPEX/Vixperation. LIke sure, options get rolled but the order of magnitude  is significantly less. If we were around 5900 the PA would make way more sense it just seems like liquidity is so thin up here that i can't figure out why  we haven't seen any sort of spike in VIX. Not talking about 30+ here. Just a small increase.

comment.body: It is really simple. Investors are selling vol. Out yourself in a dealers position. Today a bunch of customers starts selling you calls. For tomorrow you lower the price, because customers have a high demand for it. Next day comes, again customers want to sell calls. What are you going to do on the third day?

comment.body: We are in a regime, where vol is sold to dealers. This vol provides gamma. Gamma surpresses RV. The vol sold realizes a profit. Investors start it again.
comment.raw_body: We are in a regime, where vol is sold to dealers. This vol provides gamma. Gamma surpresses RV. The vol sold realizes a profit. Investors start it again.

comment.body: Because so much gamma is provided, that you do not get a -5% or black swan move. RV has to spike first, to elavate IV. Then you can get a bigger move. So there is no "nasty" move. You get a warning shot before.
comment.raw_body: Because so much gamma is provided, that you do not get a -5% or black swan move. RV has to spike first, to elavate IV. Then you can get a bigger move. So there is no "nasty" move. You get a warning shot before.

comment.body: Hey, if you sell a VIX call to the dealers it basically hits index hedging the same way as being sold a 5–10 delta put. That VIX call sale loads dealers with extra upside gamma, which mechanically soaks up any VIX spike. Like she laid out in yday’s post.. picture a short-VIX-fly gamma landscape centered on the 16.5 strike. Below that linedealers got VIX gamma, and VIX vega flips to the downside, all while SPX vanna slowly drifts into positive territory. From there, a vol-selling feedback loop kicks in, not just VIX vega...
comment.raw_body: Hey, if you sell a VIX call to the dealers it basically hits index hedging the same way as being sold a 5–10 delta put. That VIX call sale loads dealers with extra upside gamma, which mechanically soaks up any VIX spike. Like she laid out in yday’s post.. picture a short-VIX-fly gamma landscape centered on the 16.5 strike. Below that linedealers got VIX gamma, and VIX vega flips to the downside, all while SPX vanna slowly drifts into positive territory. comment.body: If i have 100 coconuts and due to opex i lose 50 coconuts then the price per coconut goes up until my supply of coconuts is refreshed assuming constant demand. comment.body:
Got the 80.6% signal on SPX rn.
But the overall pic is shady. It might foreshadows the retest of the 6080 or even 6034 line, but nothing structure-breaking ;))
Size accordingly. Or not, just wait and see, don't do anything. 
To me this is not enough yet. 
Vol selling and very small delta selling at most, a bit higher. Very small...

I don't expect anything bearish pullback until July 8.
Market is supported through July. 
The sentiment is manipulated to mask underlying recession risk.
FOMC will be a backstab, then risk down. 
We will see.
